description: Full Home Assistant control via REST API. Manage entities, call services, control automations/scripts/scenes, publish MQTT, read/write Modbus registers, render templates, inspect logs, and restart HA — all through the Home Assistant REST API with a long-lived access token. Requires ha_token secret (set via `ironclaw tool setup ha-tool`) and the HA base URL written to the workspace file at ha/base_url.

conditional_requirements: All actions require ha_token (secret) and ha_base_url (workspace file at ha/base_url). Run install.sh to configure both.
call_service requires domain and service; data is optional.
fire_event requires event_type; event_data is optional.
modbus_write requires unit, address, and value.
get_history requires entity_id; hours_back (default 24) or start_time (ISO 8601) is optional.
get_logbook accepts optional entity_id and hours_back.
notes: Use get_states to discover all entity IDs before targeting specific entities.
Use get_services to discover available service domains and service names.
MQTT is controlled via call_service with domain=mqtt, service=publish.
Modbus devices are controlled via call_service with domain=modbus.
Automations can be listed via get_states (domain filter: automation) and triggered via call_service(automation, trigger).
Scripts: list via get_states(domain=script), run via call_service(script, <script_id>).
Scenes: list via get_states(domain=scene), activate via call_service(scene, turn_on).
restart_ha will restart the Home Assistant instance — use with caution.

auth.instructions: Create a long-lived access token in Home Assistant: go to your profile page (http://your-ha-url:8123/profile), scroll to 'Long-Lived Access Tokens', and create a new token.
auth.token_hint: Long-lived access token from HA profile page

required_secrets.name: ha_token
required_secrets.prompt: Home Assistant long-lived access token (from your HA profile page)
